text: India's local governance operates through a three-tier Panchayati Raj system established by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment (1992). Three tiers: Gram Panchayat (village level), Panchayat Samiti/Block Panchayat (block level), Zilla Parishad (district level). There are approximately 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats in India. Elections are held every 5 years. 1/3 seats reserved for women. SCs/STs get proportional reservation.

Gram Sabha is the general body of all registered voters in a Panchayat area. It is the foundation of the Panchayati Raj system. Functions: approve development plans, review annual accounts, identify beneficiaries for government schemes, social audit of MGNREGA and other programs. Quorum is typically 1/10th of registered voters. Meetings held at least 2-4 times a year.

Urban local governance established by the 74th Constitutional Amendment (1992). Three types: Municipal Corporation (for large cities, headed by Mayor), Municipal Council (for smaller towns), Nagar Panchayat (for transitional areas). Functions: water supply, sanitation, roads, street lighting, public health, urban planning. Smart Cities Mission and AMRUT target urban infrastructure improvement.

Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) 2005 guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household. If employment not provided within 15 days, unemployment allowance must be paid. Current wage: Rs 200-350/day depending on state. Annual budget: Rs 86,000 crore (2024-25). Over 15 crore active job cards. Social audit is mandatory through Gram Sabha.
